front: Architect: primary architect of Brasilia, Brazil's planned capital; collaborated with urban planner Lucio Costa, who designed the airplane-shaped city layout along the Monumental Axis
Architect: collaborated with Le Corbusier on a joint submission (Plan 23/32) for the United Nations Headquarters; the two reportedly feuded during the project
Architect: won the Pritzker Prize in 1988; known for bold use of curved reinforced concrete and parabolic vaults

Architect: designed the 'Alta' lounge chair in collaboration with his daughter; in 2012 collaborated with Converse on Chuck Taylor trainers printed with a poem about his longing for the 'free and sensual curve'
Architect: designed the 2003 Serpentine Pavilion in London; also designed 'The Volcano' cultural center in Le Havre, France
Architect: designed the Palacio da Alvorada (presidential residence in Brasilia) and the Brasilia Palace hotel
Building: crown-shaped cathedral built from sixteen hyperbolic concrete columns that curve outward and support a glass roof; three angels suspended by steel cables inside
Building: dedicated to Our Lady of Aparecida, patroness of Brazil; partially cooled by a reflecting pool on its roof
Building: topped by two white domes — one facing up, one facing down (a bowl and a dome) — symbolizing the bicameral legislature; lies along Brasilia's Monumental Axis
Building: commissioned by President Juscelino Kubitschek; Roberto Burle Marx designed the surrounding gardens
Building: part of the Pampulha Project (a suburb complex around a man-made lagoon in Belo Horizonte); made of four concrete parabolas and an inverted obelisk
back: Church of Saint Francis of Assisi, Pampulha (Oscar Niemeyer)
work: Church of Saint Francis of Assisi (Pampulha)

back: Church of Saint Francis of Assisi, Pampulha (Oscar Niemeyer)
work: Church of Saint Francis of Assisi (Pampulha)

front: Building: local bishop called it 'the devil's bomb shelter'; hanger-inspired style scandalized the clergy and delayed its use by a decade
back: Church of Saint Francis of Assisi, Pampulha (Oscar Niemeyer)
work: Church of Saint Francis of Assisi (Pampulha)

front: Building: exterior blue mosaics and murals by quasi-cubist painter Candido Portinari; contains a tapestry copy of Picasso's Guernica inside
back: Church of Saint Francis of Assisi, Pampulha (Oscar Niemeyer)
work: Church of Saint Francis of Assisi (Pampulha)

front: Building: saucer-shaped contemporary art museum in Niteroi likened to a UFO; set on a cliffside above a reflecting pool; reached by a winding red-carpeted ramp
Building: large residential tower in São Paulo with a distinctive S-shaped (sinuous) facade made of 72 million exterior tiles
Building: self-designed namesake museum in Curitiba (capital of Parana state); features a wide eye-shaped tower nicknamed 'the Eye' balanced atop a single yellow rectangular-tiled pillar
Building: designed while in political exile in Paris; features a massive underground 'Worker's Hall'; done as a favor for the party
